title: CommonJS seed file cannot run under package-level ES module mode
reasoning: The package declares all .js files as ES modules, but src/seeds/seed.js uses CommonJS globals and exports. Executing or importing this file as Node source will fail before seeding starts because require and module are not defined in ES module scope.
reproduction: Run node src/seeds/seed.js in a fresh checkout with the current package.json; Node treats the file as ESM because of type=module and throws a require/module scope error.
recommendation: Either convert the seed script to native ESM imports/exports and ESM main detection, or rename it to .cjs and ensure any callers reference the new filename.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests or npm scripts exercise src/seeds/seed.js, so the module-format mismatch is not caught by the declared lint/build workflow.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a smoke test or npm script that imports or executes the seed entrypoint in the same Node module mode used by the package.
minimumFixScope: Change src/seeds/seed.js module syntax or file extension, plus any package/script references that invoke it.
